spec_match.rationale: Captures the type-product rules — length × number = length, length / length = number, angle + length = error.

interactive.rationale: 4 interactive markers — table-driven probe with live evaluation.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises the full matrix in one table; the page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: The matrix is enumerated but doesn't show all the spec cases — e.g. resolution × number, frequency / time, color in modern calc, etc.

openQuestions.title: Add resolution / frequency / time rows
openQuestions.detail: The CSS Values spec includes time, frequency, resolution as dimensioned types. Add rows so the matrix is complete — currently only length/angle are covered.

openQuestions.title: Show the resulting CSS error
openQuestions.detail: For invalid combinations the page should show the actual CSS parser behaviour (`calc(1px + 1deg)` produces `invalid`). Currently the table likely just says 'error' — surface the real fallback (CSS rule discarded? value computed as invalid?).

summary: Cleanest reference card in the set — table-driven probe that's actually queryable. To complete, add the other dimensioned types and surface the real CSS error path.
